article_meta.title: Jurisprudence People Are Taking the Wrong Lesson From This Weekend's Dual Massacres By Ido Kirson and Austin Sarat Dec 1

reasoning: The assistant avoided attribution violations and flagged genuine author-prose bias, but missed multiple clear instances and misapplied at least one bias type.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the authors’ own prose; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged item reflects a defensible bias pattern (asserting policy efficacy as fact; emotionally loaded advocacy; an un-attributed factual claim).
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several prominent author-prose biases, including: 'That's the wrong question' (spin), 'It is clear that avenues exist for Congress to regulate firearms' (opinion as fact), 'We need our own version of Australia's National Firearms Agreement' (advocacy/opinion), and labeling the Court as 'pro-gun' (subjective qualifier).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Item 1 is well-labeled. Item 2 is better categorized as Sensationalism/Emotionalism or Opinion Statements Presented as Fact rather than Slant. Item 3 is arguably Omission of Source Attribution but could have used a direct sourcing critique; label is defensible.
